summary: No-ship: the new guards reduce stale rows and scan work, but same-boot PID reuse and history-scale recency/bounding failures remain material.

findings.title: Same-boot PID reuse still passes the live-registry guard
findings.recommendation: Do not treat filename-pid equality plus process.kill(pid, 0) as ownership proof. A crashed same-boot Claude file can be revived when that pid is reused by any live process. Verify the OS process command/exe and start time against startedAt, or require a fresh updatedAt heartbeat TTL before returning the row.

findings.title: maxScan can skip the newest history rows
findings.recommendation: Do not spend the scan budget based on parent directory mtime or unsorted readdir order. Parent directory mtime does not change when an existing transcript is appended, and readdir order is arbitrary, so active transcripts can sit behind the maxScan cutoff. Use a recency index/cache or stat enough candidates to compute a true top-K before truncating.

findings.title: maxScan still does unbounded directory work
findings.recommendation: The code stats every project directory and loads each visited directory's full entry list before maxScan is checked. Bound project-dir stats and entry reads too, for example with an indexed cache or incremental fs.opendir iteration with explicit budgets.

findings.title: Large transcripts can lose the only ai-title outside the tail window
findings.recommendation: Fixed head/tail sampling cannot prove latest ai-title. For files larger than the tail window, if the tail contains no ai-title, scan backwards in chunks until one is found or return an explicit unknown title instead of falling back to the first user prompt while an unsampled ai-title may exist.
